title: pilosella brachycoma subsp. acrochaetia
intro: Pilosella brachycoma subsp. acrochaetia is a lesser-known subspecies of the Pilosella genus, commonly known as 'Mouse-ear Hawkweed' or 'Pilosella'. It is a type of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, making it useful for treating various health issues, including wounds, infections, and digestive problems. In herbal preparations, Pilosella brachycoma subsp. acrochaetia is often used as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system.
